title: momordica henriquesii
intro: Momordica henriquesii, a species of the Momordica genus, is a perennial climber native to the tropical regions of West Africa. It is also known as the "West African bitter apple" and is often used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Momordica henriquesii include its potential to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as an antimalarial agent. Herbal preparations of Momordica henriquesii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
